impact:
PARTIALLY FIXED by ISS-008 (2026-07-16). Re-measure before doing anything else here.

*** THE ORIGINAL DIAGNOSIS WAS WRONG AND IS RETRACTED. *** This was recorded as "+6 dB at BL=0.65 is
NodalCircuit impedance loading — NOT fixable by a scalar". It was, in fact, exactly a scalar:
kDryGain[1] = 2.534 multiplied V1L's dry/wet ratio by +8.1 dB (kDryGain = kInputRef/kOutputMakeup, a
per-path fudge applied only to the dry leg — see ISS-008 for why that is unphysical). Removing it
moved the V1L partial-blend captures:
    BL=0.65: raw clean null -9.6 -> -12.7 dB
    BL=0.30: raw clean null -1.9 ->  -4.1 dB
while both V1L BL=1.00 captures were unchanged — the signature of a dry-leg-only error. Note the
retracted explanation predicted the opposite (a scalar could not fix it); do not resurrect it without
new evidence.

WHAT REMAINS. V1L BL=0.65 FR rms is still 7.45 dB and BL=0.30 is 9.55 dB, so a real residual survives
— but it is now confounded almost entirely by ISS-009, not by the blend.

STILL BLOCKED BY ISS-009, for the original reason (unchanged): this residual is measured against a
V1L WET path that is -12.9 dB LF-deficient, so BLEND mixes dry against a broken wet path and the two
cannot be separated. cascade_analysis.py section C's "adding dry partially CANCELS the wet path's LF
deficit" reading still stands.

WHAT CHANGED FOR THE SEQUENCE. The old plan was "re-measure after ISS-009 -> re-fit kOutputMakeup[1]
-> re-derive kDryGain[1]". kDryGain NO LONGER EXISTS and must not be reintroduced (ISS-008). The
dry/wet ratio is now set by the circuit alone, which is correct and is not a fit target. kOutputMakeup
is a separate, purely cosmetic global scalar — see ISS-012.

Next: land ISS-009, then re-measure this from scratch. The remaining residual may be small enough to
close outright.
title: V1L blend residual at partial BLEND settings
